prompt:
You judge yoga drop-in class pages.

A visitor wants to answer: "When can I come? How much? Where? What styles?"

Good page:
- Class schedule visible near the top — day, time, style
- Prices clear (single class, class packs, first-time deals)
- Studio address and how to get there
- Easy way to book or just show up
- Short class descriptions, not long philosophy

Bad page:
- Schedule hidden below a long story
- "What is yoga" text on a commercial class page
- Prices missing or "contact us for prices"
- Training and retreat offers mixed in with drop-in classes

SEO (Google) — check:
- Page title names the city and class type (e.g. "Hatha classes in Barcelona")
- Clear H1 matching the title
- LocalBusiness schema with address, phone, hours
- Unique copy, not generic boilerplate
- Images have alt text

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of what the page offers>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, how well does the page help a visitor book?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, how well can Google find and rank the page?>",
  "classStyles": ["<yoga style>", "..."],
  "pricingVisible": true/false,
  "scheduleVisible": true/false,
  "firstTimeDeal": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
If the category description specifies a JSON schema, follow it exactly.
Otherwise, return fields you find relevant based on the category description.
Every record must always include "url" (the page URL) and "summary" (1-2 sentence plain-language overview of the page).
Fields ending in "Score" must be numbers from 1 to 10.
Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.shalastudios.com/schedule",
      "pageName": "SHA-LA Schedule",
      "summary": "Weekly class schedule page for SHA-LA Studios in Berlin with filters for location, teacher, and class type, but actual class listings appear to be loaded via an external widget (Yogo) that did not render in the crawl.",
      "conversionScore": 4,
      "seoScore": 4,
      "classStyles": [],
      "pricingVisible": false,
      "scheduleVisible": false,
      "firstTimeDeal": false,
      "notes": "The schedule relies entirely on a third-party Yogo widget that didn't render, leaving no visible class times, styles, or prices — a visitor sees almost nothing useful without JavaScript fully loading."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.shalastudios.com/book-class",
      "pageName": "SHA-LA Book a Class / Pricing",
      "summary": "Pricing and booking page for SHA-LA Studios Berlin listing all pass options — drop-in (€20), 5-class pack (€85), 10-class pack (€149), week pass (€48), and a 2-week trial pass (€49) for first-timers — with direct buy links.",
      "conversionScore": 8,
      "seoScore": 5,
      "classStyles": [],
      "pricingVisible": true,
      "scheduleVisible": false,
      "firstTimeDeal": true,
      "notes": "Pricing is clear and well-structured with a visible first-timer trial deal, but class styles are never mentioned and the page title/H1 lacks city or style keywords that would help SEO."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.shalastudios.com/studios",
      "pageName": "SHA-LA Studios Locations",
      "summary": "Location page describing SHA-LA's two Berlin studios — Prenzlauer Berg (Kastanienallee 63) and Kreuzberg Waterfront (Falckensteinstr 47) — with addresses, directions links, and studio amenities.",
      "conversionScore": 6,
      "seoScore": 6,
      "classStyles": [],
      "pricingVisible": false,
      "scheduleVisible": false,
      "firstTimeDeal": false,
      "notes": "Addresses and directions are clearly provided which is helpful, but there are no class styles, prices, or schedule on this page, and the studio rental section adds noise for drop-in visitors."
    }
  ]
}
